title: Classes directly extending object should not call base in GetHashCode or Equals
description: Classes directly extending "object" should not call "base" in "GetHashCode" or "Equals"Analyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoMaking abasecall when overriding a method is generally a good idea, but not in the case ofGetHashCodeandEqualsfor classes that directly extendObject. These methods are based on the object’s reference, meaning that no two objects that use thosebasemethods can be equal or have the same hash.ExceptionsThis rule doesn’t report on guard conditions checking for reference equality. For example:public override bool Equals(object obj) { if (base.Equals(obj)) // Compliant, it's a guard condition.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
